title: hieracium apophyadium
parts: Hieracium apophyadium has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, flowers, and roots, which are used in traditional medicine. The leaves of Hieracium apophyadium contain flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have anti-inflammatory properties. The flowers of Hieracium apophyadium contain sesquiterpene lactones, including helenalin and iso-helenalin, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities. The roots of Hieracium apophyadium contain saponins, including helenin and helenalin, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever and digestive issues.
side_effects: Hieracium apophyadium can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing severe gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxins can also lead to kidney damage and liver inflammation. In some cases, improper use of Hieracium apophyadium may cause allergic reactions, resulting in skin irritation, itching, and respiratory distress. Furthermore, excessive consumption of the plant's extracts can disrupt the balance of gut bacteria, leading to long-term digestive problems.
